channel.title: Coding Literacy Course
channel.description: A conceptual course covering how code works, how codebases are organised, version control, APIs, cloud deployment, enterprise software, AI/vibe coding, home server setup, and security.

item.title: 01: What Code Is and How It Runs
item.description: Source code, compilation, interpretation, runtimes, and what an IDE actually is.

item.title: 02: Major Programming Languages
item.description: Python, JavaScript, C/C++, Java, SQL, Rust, Go, Swift and Kotlin - what each is for and why it matters.

item.title: 03: How Codebases Are Organised
item.description: Files, modules, packages, dependencies, and what package managers actually do.

item.title: 04: Version Control - Git and GitHub
item.description: What version control solves, how Git works conceptually, and why GitHub won.

item.title: 05: How Software Talks to Other Software
item.description: APIs, REST, databases, and what frontend and backend actually mean.

item.title: 06: The Cloud and Deployment
item.description: What deploying means, servers, containers, and how CI/CD automates shipping code.

item.title: 07: Enterprise-Level Software
item.description: Microservices, technical debt, code review culture, security, and secrets management.

item.title: 08: Where AI and Vibe Coding Fit
item.description: Where LLM-generated code sits in the stack, why context still matters, and what can go wrong.

item.title: 09: What's Running on Your Pi
item.description: Processes, services, ports, and what Jellyfin and your torrent client are actually doing.

item.title: 10: How Home Networks Work
item.description: Routers, local vs external IP, NAT, and what is and isn't visible from the internet.

item.title: 11: The Realistic Threat Model
item.description: What the actual risks are for a home server, what aren't, and how to think about it.

item.title: 12: SSH and How to Harden It
item.description: What SSH is, key-based auth, disabling root login, and what fail2ban does.

item.title: 13: Ports and Firewalls
item.description: What ports are open by default, what a firewall does, and what port forwarding exposes.

item.title: 14: VPNs - The Full Picture
item.description: How VPNs work, what they do and don't protect, kill switches, and choosing the right provider.

item.title: 15: Jellyfin Security
item.description: Authentication, external access, reverse proxies, and HTTPS for your media server.

item.title: 16: Torrent Client Considerations
item.description: Why torrent traffic benefits from VPN and how to bind your client to the VPN interface.

item.title: 17: General Hardening
item.description: Keeping software updated, user permissions, sudo, and disabling unnecessary services.

item.title: 18: Practical Monitoring
item.description: What to monitor, Netdata, reading auth logs, and knowing when something is wrong.
